notes.content: The UC Davis Bachelor of Science in Business offers a core foundation anchored in math, statistics and economics while building competencies in fundamental areas of business. Business majors choose to specialize in one or two of the following areas: Accounting Finance Marketing & Business Analytics Management & Strategy The major is a first-of-its-kind campus partnership between the Graduate School of Management, College of Letters & Science, and College of Agricultural & Environmental Sciences. The Graduate School of Management awards the Bachelor of Science in Business.
notes.contentHtml:
The UC Davis Bachelor of Science in Business offers a core foundation anchored in math, statistics and economics while building competencies in fundamental areas of business. Business majors choose to specialize in one or two of the following areas:

  • Accounting
  • Finance
  • Marketing & Business Analytics
  • Management & Strategy

The major is a first-of-its-kind campus partnership between the Graduate School of Management, College of Letters & Science, and College of Agricultural & Environmental Sciences. The Graduate School of Management awards the Bachelor of Science in Business.

notes.content: Business majors often go on to pursue careers in diverse fields, including accounting, finance, sales, marketing, consulting, business analytics, entrepreneurship, and human resources. Additionally, graduates may pursue advanced degrees in areas such as business administration (MBA), accounting, finance, public policy, and law.
notes.contentHtml: Business majors often go on to pursue careers in diverse fields, including accounting, finance, sales, marketing, consulting, business analytics, entrepreneurship, and human resources. Additionally, graduates may pursue advanced degrees in areas such as business administration (MBA), accounting, finance, public policy, and law.

notes.content: Open for transfer students starting Fall 2027 This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission. Important Information: Complete the courses below and maintain a 3.2 GPA across the entire group: ● Economics (ECN) 001A/B ● Management (MGT) 011A/B ● Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B/C or 019A/B/C or 021A/B/C ● Statistics (STAT) 013 or 032 Visit Department agreements as needed Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions ( Find my advisor ) Note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination: AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog . Quick reference charts for AP and IB.
notes.contentHtml:
Open for transfer students starting Fall 2027


This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.


Important Information:


Complete the courses below and maintain a 3.2 GPA across the entire group:

●    Economics (ECN) 001A/B

●    Management (MGT) 011A/B

●    Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B/C or 019A/B/C or 021A/B/C

●    Statistics (STAT) 013 or 032


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

notes.content: This major is not available via TAG . All students interested in applying to the Business major may still submit the transfer UC application in November.
